claim: Chatterbox TTS is a 500M parameter open-source model trained on 500K hours of clean audio; it enables zero-shot voice cloning with 5-20 seconds of reference audio
actionable: Use Chatterbox for open-source voice cloning; it's smaller than competitors (Dia, Gemini TTS) and provides local execution without API costs
source_title: Building with Chatterbox TTS, Voice Cloning & Watermarking

claim: Chatterbox voice cloning conditions on reference audio to transfer timbre, tonality, and reverb; it retains speaker characteristics while generating new speech
actionable: Provide clean reference audio for voice cloning; understand that the model conditions on the audio rather than perfectly replicating the voice
source_title: Building with Chatterbox TTS, Voice Cloning & Watermarking

claim: Exaggeration control in Chatterbox allows tuning emotional intensity of speech; higher exaggeration values can cause increased speech speed if CFG weight is not reduced correspondingly
actionable: Balance exaggeration and CFG weight; when increasing exaggeration, reduce CFG weight to maintain natural speech speed
source_title: Building with Chatterbox TTS, Voice Cloning & Watermarking

claim: Chatterbox generates ~40 seconds of audio per pass; segment longer texts into 30-second chunks to avoid truncation
actionable: Split long-form text into 30-second segments for Chatterbox; process in sections for audiobooks or long-form content
source_title: Building with Chatterbox TTS, Voice Cloning & Watermarking

claim: Chatterbox includes watermarking capability to detect synthetic audio; watermarked outputs are identifiable as AI-generated, providing provenance and deepfake detection
actionable: Use watermarking for accountability; enable detection of AI-generated audio in production systems
source_title: Building with Chatterbox TTS, Voice Cloning & Watermarking

claim: Chatterbox can produce audio artifacts at segment boundaries (e.g., 'twos to speech' instead of 'text to speech'); review generated audio for edge cases
actionable: Listen to generated audio for artifacts; manually fix boundary issues or re-segment if necessary
source_title: Building with Chatterbox TTS, Voice Cloning & Watermarking

claim: Chatterbox TTS Extended (open-source) enables batch processing of text files, custom output formats, and optional watermark disabling for production use
actionable: Use Chatterbox TTS Extended for production audio generation; batch-process files instead of single-call API patterns
source_title: Building with Chatterbox TTS, Voice Cloning & Watermarking

claim: Chatterbox quality is lower than Gemini TTS but offers better control and local execution compared to proprietary solutions like ElevenLabs
actionable: Choose Chatterbox for control and cost; choose Gemini TTS if highest quality is required; avoid ElevenLabs for cost-sensitive applications
source_title: Building with Chatterbox TTS, Voice Cloning & Watermarking
